meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Struggling with project ideas? Learn to build advanced email automation using Antigravity and Claude Code. A high-level checklist for student developers.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. Build an automated email engine that scrapes, processes, and sends personalized newsletters based on real-time data. This project proves you can handle API orchestration, data pipelines, and intelligent content generation—the exact stack employers look for in top-tier junior engineers. Start building your portfolio today.
content.intro:
The screen is blank, the cursor is blinking, and the dreaded 'tutorial hell' has set in. You have the skills to build, but no idea what to build—a paralyzing gap that keeps thousands of students from ever shipping their first production-ready application. You don't need a revolutionary startup idea; you need a technical challenge that proves your ability to orchestrate complex systems.

Email automation is the silent workhorse of the modern internet. By building a system that fetches, filters, and delivers intelligent content, you transition from 'code-along' student to 'system architect.' We are going to bypass the noise and build a professional-grade pipeline using Antigravity and Claude Code. This isn't just a project; it's your ticket to demonstrating engineering maturity.

steps.title: Define the Data Pipeline Schema
steps.description: Establish the structure of your incoming data. You need a robust schema to handle API responses before they hit your email engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python Pydantic model for an email newsletter item containing title, url, summary, and sentiment_score. Include a validation method for cleaning raw HTML content.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.py --spec 'Pydantic model for newsletter items'

steps.title: Configure the Scraper Engine
steps.description: Use an automated fetcher to pull data from your chosen source (RSS, API, or web scraper).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular scraper function using httpx that fetches data from a provided RSS feed URL and returns a list of dictionaries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file scraper.py --logic 'async fetch and parse RSS feeds'

steps.title: Implement LLM-Based Summarization
steps.description: Integrate Claude to transform raw data into highly personalized, readable summaries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a prompt template for Claude that takes a list of tech articles and summarizes them into three actionable bullet points per article.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'integrate Anthropic API for summarization logic'

steps.title: Set Up the Email Dispatcher
steps.description: Connect to an SMTP relay or API like Resend to ship your automated content to a target inbox.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function to send an HTML-formatted email using the Resend API, including error handling for failed network requests.

steps.title: Orchestrate the Workflow
steps.description: Tie everything together into a main execution loop with error logging and scheduling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a main.py file that initializes the scraper, processes data through the LLM, and triggers the mailer, wrapped in a try-except block.

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for API keys; never hardcode credentials, even in local development.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode that logs output to the console instead of sending real emails during testing.
Add a simple database like SQLite to track sent articles and prevent duplicate emails to the same user.
Use structured logging instead of print statements to debug production pipeline failures effectively.
Rate-limit your API calls to avoid hitting vendor quotas and incurring unexpected costs.
content.next_steps: Deploy your automation script to a cloud function (like AWS Lambda or Vercel) and schedule it using a CRON job to make it fully autonomous.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build systems that scale. Stop dreaming about projects and start shipping the infrastructure that powers them. The industry is waiting for engineers who can bridge the gap between AI and real-world utility. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
